pages.raw_text:
The striking brewers, bottlers and
drivers, employed by the Spokane
Brewing u Mailing company, to the
number of p, asked the trades council
last night to assist them in their fight
against the big brewing firm and de-
clare 1t unfair to union labor. After
a heated debate, the matter was post
poned tor definite action until tonight.
In the meantime the committee ap-
pointed last is confering with n
committee of brewery owners looking
toward an amicable settlement of diff
ticultes. This committee will report
to the trades council at the adjourned
meeting held tonight.


The strikers have asked the bartend-
ers to walk out in every saloon where
the beer made by the Spokane Brewing
4 Mailing company is used. They
hardly expect this last request to be
complied with. A prominent member
of the bartenders' union said today
that the bartenders would hardly take
up the fight in the interests of the
brewery employees for two reasons:
First. II is not a question of wages;
and second, when the bartenders were
fighting the Statue and Carson saloons
on the north side and other places last
summer, the union brewery drivers
still continued to haul these places
union made beer. Consequently there
was generated much i'll will between
the two organizations, which has con
tinued to exist ever since that time.


Might Lose Their Jobs.


There are over 200 union bartenders
in the city. Many of these realize
that going ont on strike will cost them
